Plaintiff Gary Kovacs' Motion For Sanctions Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 2023.030 For Violations Of The April 2, 2018 Discovery Order
Off Calendar Jul-20-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Romeo, Esq. 949-798-1316 of Crowell Moring for Plaintiff Gary Kovacs.
Earle Miller, Esq. 213-620-7785 of White & Case for Defendant AVG.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues. The Court grants Plaintiff's request to specially set a Discovery motion on the 9:30 law and Motion calendar. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff Gary Kovacs' Motion For Sanctions Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 2023.030 For Violations Of The April 2, 2018 Discovery Order.

Plaintiff's motion is DENIED. Rather than seek production of the few documents still at issue, plaintiff seeks issue sanctions that are effectively case-dispositive or sweeping evidentiary sanctions. Both proposed sets of sanctions are extreme, draconian and unwarranted. No monetary sanctions are awarded.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Evidentiary & Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Avg Technologies Usa, Inc. Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production & For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark A. Romeo, Esq. of Crowell Moring, T:949.798.1316, for plaintiff/moving party;
Bryan a. Merryman, Esq. of White & Case LLP, T:213.620.7802, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF GARY KOVACS' Motion For Evidentiary & Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant AVG Technologies USA, Inc. Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production & For Monetary Sanctions, as follows:

Requests 4, 36, 35: DENIED; the Court's December 7, 2017 discovery order did not require a supplemental written response to 4 or 36 and did not mention 35. Requests 15, 19, 26: GRANTED; unredacted copies of the at-issue documents are to be produced, because AVG "intends to use" in this litigation "the facts obtained in the investigation" of plaintiff as well as the "interview[s]." (12/7/17 Order pp. 2, 5.) Evidentiary and monetary sanctions are DENIED; the motion papers do not comply with the specificity requirements of CCP 2023.040.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.
